info.title: AISee Orchestrator
info.description: Task scheduling and coordination service for AISee platform

post.description: Create a new task for a product
Start AEO analysis for a product with complete task orchestration

Start AEO analysis for a task with complete task orchestration.
Handles different scenarios based on task status:
- In-progress tasks: Return error (cannot re-execute while running)
- Fully completed root tasks: Return error (suggest using start_product_analysis)
- Other scenarios: Delegate to execute_task_copy_analysis

This ensures all token consumption (token_success, token_failed) and billing
records are clearly preserved for each execution attempt.
Callback for AEO analysis - handles A2A push notifications

Get the latest task for a product with its children recursively. Supports both product UUID and website URL.

Get all tasks for a user as hierarchical tree structure with dynamic filters.
Returns only root tasks (parent_id IS NULL) and original tasks (original_task_id IS NULL) to avoid duplicates.
Use get_task_executions() to get all executions (original + copies) for a specific task.

Get all executions (original + copies) for a task, ordered by creation time.
Uses original_task_id index for efficient querying.

put.description: Update task status and progress with pessimistic locking to prevent race conditions

post.description:
Estimate the credit cost for executing a task tree.

Supports two scenarios:
1. New task from template: Provide task_template_id and product_id
2. Task copy (re-execution): Provide task_id (will simulate create_task_copy logic)

Args:
    request: TaskCostEstimateRequest with either:
        - task_template_id and product_id (for new task scenario)
        - task_id (for task copy scenario)

Returns:
    - estimated_cost: Total estimated credit cost
    - task_breakdown: List of tasks with their individual estimated costs
    - user_available_credits: User's current available credit balance
    - sufficient_credits: Whether user has sufficient credits

get.description: Get task limits and current status for a user
Get task health summary

post.description: Run task health check to detect and retry stuck tasks

Fix inconsistent task status caused by previous task copy bugs.

Finds and fixes two types of inconsistencies:
1. Vertical dependencies: root tasks marked as 'completed' but having pending children
2. Horizontal dependencies: tasks marked as 'completed' but their dependencies are not completed

Args:
    min_age_minutes: Only fix tasks older than this many minutes

This is a data repair operation that should be run manually when needed.

get.description:
Estimate the credit cost of generating execution tasks for an Action.

Builds the same Task tree that ``generate-tasks`` would dispatch (without
saving anything) and runs ``calculate_task_tree_estimated_cost`` against it.
Reuses the model-pricing config so the number is consistent with the actual
freeze that ``execute_task_analysis`` will perform later.

Returns the EXACT same shape as ``POST /task/estimate-cost`` so the FE can
reuse its cost-breakdown renderer without any branching:
    {
      "estimated_cost":          "<decimal>",
      "task_breakdown":          [{task_code, task_name, model,
                                   estimated_tokens, pricing,
                                   estimated_cost}, ...],
      "user_available_credits":  "<decimal>",
      "sufficient_credits":      true|false
    }

schema.title: Response Estimate Action Task Cost Action  Action Id  Estimate Cost Get

post.description:
Trigger asynchronous AI generation of actionable tasks for this action.

POST returns one of these terminal/active status payloads:
  * ``{"status": "completed",   "tasks": [...]}``  — already-cached success
  * ``{"status": "processing",  "task_id": ...}``  — freshly-dispatched OR
    cache-hit on the in-flight sentinel. Client should poll
    ``GET /action/{id}/generated-tasks`` until the status flips.
  * ``{"status": "unnecessary", ...}``  — pre-flight: action is already at
    target. No LLM ran, no charge.
  * ``{"status": "unsupported", ...}``  — pre-flight: gap exists but no
    playbook coverage. No LLM ran, no charge.

POST never returns ``"failed"`` or ``"pending"`` directly — a previously
persisted error envelope falls through to a fresh dispatch (which may
succeed or fail again), and ``"pending"`` is the FE-facing label for
actions whose ``solution_data`` is still NULL (handled by GET, not POST).

Status code overrides:
  * 402 Payment Required — credit freeze rejected the dispatch
    (insufficient balance or task-limit guard). The error envelope is
    still persisted on the action so the next call retries once the user
    tops up.

Concurrency:
  The action row is locked with ``SELECT ... FOR UPDATE`` to serialize
  concurrent dispatch attempts. The lock is held until the in-flight
  sentinel is committed inside ``ActionTaskService.generate_execution_tasks``,
  after which subsequent waiting requests read the sentinel and return
  ``processing`` instead of re-dispatching. This is the only thing
  preventing two simultaneous POSTs from double-charging the user — do not
  remove the ``with_for_update()`` call below without rethinking the
  freeze pipeline.

parameters.name: content_days

schema.description: Number of days of ready-to-publish social media posts to generate (1 post per day). Set to 0 to disable CONTENT-task generation. Ignored when the action's gap is not a social-media gap.
schema.title: Content Days

parameters.description: Number of days of ready-to-publish social media posts to generate (1 post per day). Set to 0 to disable CONTENT-task generation. Ignored when the action's gap is not a social-media gap.

schema.title: Response Generate Action Tasks Action  Action Id  Generate Tasks Post

get.description:
Read-only polling endpoint: return the current state of an action's
generation task without triggering any dispatch.

Use this for high-frequency polling once a generation has been kicked off
via ``POST /action/{id}/generate-tasks``. Unlike the POST endpoint:
  * No row-level lock is acquired (cheap, replica-safe)
  * Never mutates state, never freezes credit, never creates a Task
  * Never auto-retries on prior failures — the FE must POST again

Response — one of 6 status values:
  * ``{"status": "pending"}``                       — never dispatched yet
    (action.solution_data is NULL); FE shows "Generate" button
  * ``{"status": "processing", "task_id": ...}``    — generation in flight
  * ``{"status": "completed",  "tasks": [...]}``    — generation succeeded
    (``tasks`` may be empty if the agent decided no fix was needed)
  * ``{"status": "unnecessary", ...}``              — pre-flight: action
    is already at or above target. Positive state, no error.
  * ``{"status": "unsupported", ...}``              — pre-flight: gap
    exists but no playbook coverage. Manual review needed, no error.
  * ``{"status": "failed", "error": {..., terminal: bool}}`` — true error;
    ``terminal=true`` means do NOT show retry button

schema.title: Response Get Generated Tasks Action  Action Id  Generated Tasks Get

get.description: Get feedback details

Check if a URL is reachable and accessible.

Logic:
- If URL has valid protocol (http/https), check directly
- If no protocol, try https first, then http if https fails

Examples:
- example.com: tries https://example.com first, then http://example.com
- https://example.com: checks https://example.com directly
- http://example.com: checks http://example.com directly

Returns detailed information about URL accessibility including:
- Whether the URL is accessible
- HTTP status code
- Response time
- Final URL (after redirects)
- Content type and other headers
- Which protocol worked (if multiple were tried)
Check if a model is valid. Only allows models matching provider/model format.

post.description:
Create a new subscription using Stripe Checkout (redirect-based payment).

This creates a Stripe Checkout Session and returns a URL for redirect.
After payment, users are redirected back to success_url or cancel_url.
Subscription will be created automatically when webhook confirms payment success.

If user already has an active subscription, this endpoint will return an error
directing them to use the /customer-portal endpoint to manage their subscription.

Note: Only USD currency is supported for payments.
Requires stripe_price_id to be configured in credit_package.data.

schema.title: Response Checkout Subscription Subscription Checkout Post

post.description:
Create a Stripe Customer Portal session for managing existing subscription.

The Customer Portal allows customers to manage their subscriptions through Stripe's hosted portal,
including:
- Cancel subscription (at period end or immediately)
- Resume cancelled subscription
- Change subscription plan (upgrade/downgrade)
- Update payment method
- View invoices
- Update billing information

Supported actions via flow_data:
- "cancel": Direct link to cancel subscription page
- "update_confirm": Preselect target plan and show confirmation page (requires target_product_code and target_interval)
  This is ideal when you have your own pricing page. The customer selects a plan on your site,
  then uses this flow to confirm the update with Stripe handling payment failures, 3D Secure, etc.
- "update": Let customer select a plan from available plans in the Customer Portal
  This allows customers to browse and select from plans configured in Stripe Customer Portal settings.
  Useful for allowing customers to change plans or cancel scheduled changes directly in the portal.
- None: Normal portal access (user can manually cancel/resume/update)

All changes made in the portal will be synchronized to the local database via webhooks.

Note: This endpoint requires the user to have an active subscription. If no subscription exists,
use the /checkout endpoint to create a new subscription first.

Reference: https://docs.stripe.com/customer-management/portal-deep-links

schema.title: Response Create Customer Portal Session Subscription Customer Portal Post

post.description:
Handle Stripe webhook events.

Supported events:
- payment_intent.succeeded: One-time payment succeeded (for credit packages)
- payment_intent.payment_failed: One-time payment failed (for credit packages)
- payment_intent.created: Informational event (no action needed, payment not completed yet)
- invoice.payment_succeeded: Subscription payment succeeded (initial or renewal)
- invoice.payment_failed: Subscription payment failed (renewal or initial)
- customer.subscription.deleted: Subscription cancelled
- customer.subscription.updated: Subscription updated (e.g., cancel_at_period_end changed)

Event Flow:
- payment_intent.created: PaymentIntent created (informational, no action)
- payment_intent.succeeded: Payment completed successfully → Create transaction & grant credits
- payment_intent.payment_failed: Payment failed → Update transaction status to FAILED

Note: Webhook secret should be configured in environment variables.

post.description:
Deduct credits from a user's balance (phase 1).

Consumes credits immediately and creates a transaction with status
**CONFIRMED**.  The caller must later call ``POST /deduct/confirm``
to finalise the transaction to SUCCESS or FAILED.

Creates debt if balance is insufficient (service is not interrupted).
Idempotent: duplicate task_id returns the previous result without
re-deducting.

Finalise a credit deduction (phase 2).

Transitions the transaction from CONFIRMED to SUCCESS or FAILED.

On SUCCESS: records consumption in UserConsumptionTick for analytics.
On FAILED: restores consumed credits (refunded) to the user.

Idempotent: calling with the same task_id + status is a no-op.

expiry_days.title: Expiry Days
expiry_days.description: Expiry days (null = permanent). For subscriptions and top-ups, must be None. For bonus packages, can be set to number of days.

return_url.title: Return Url
return_url.description: URL to redirect customer to after they finish managing their subscription. If not provided, FRONTEND_URL from settings will be used. For update_confirm action, this is also used as after_completion redirect if after_completion_redirect_url is not provided.

target_product_code.description: Target product code for plan update (e.g., 'starter-month', 'developer-year'). Required when action is 'update_confirm'.

target_interval.title: Target Interval
target_interval.description: Target billing interval ('month' or 'year'). Required when action is 'update_confirm' and must match target_product_code.

after_completion_redirect_url.title: After Completion Redirect Url
after_completion_redirect_url.description: URL to redirect customer to after successfully completing the flow. Only used for update_confirm action. If not provided, return_url will be used instead.

promotion_code.title: Promotion Code
promotion_code.description: Promotion code to apply during subscription update. Only used for update_confirm action.

CustomerPortalRequest.title: CustomerPortalRequest
CustomerPortalRequest.description:
Customer Portal request for managing existing subscription

The Customer Portal allows customers to manage their subscriptions through Stripe's hosted portal,
including canceling, resuming, changing plans, updating payment methods, and viewing invoices.

Supported actions via flow_data:
- "cancel": Direct link to cancel subscription page
- "update_confirm": Preselect target plan and show confirmation page (requires target_product_code and target_interval)
  This is ideal when you have your own pricing page. The customer selects a plan on your site,
  then uses this flow to confirm the update with Stripe handling payment failures, 3D Secure, etc.
- "update": Let customer select a plan from available plans in the Customer Portal
  This allows customers to browse and select from plans configured in Stripe Customer Portal settings.
  Useful for allowing customers to change plans or cancel scheduled changes directly in the portal.
- None: Normal portal access (user can manually cancel/resume/update)

For update_confirm action:
- If after_completion_redirect_url is provided, customer will be redirected there after successful update
- If after_completion_redirect_url is not provided, return_url will be used for after_completion redirect
- You can optionally specify promotion_code to apply a discount during the update

For update action:
- Customer can select from available plans configured in Stripe Customer Portal settings
- If after_completion_redirect_url is provided, customer will be redirected there after successful update
- If after_completion_redirect_url is not provided, return_url will be used for after_completion redirect
- Useful for allowing customers to change plans or cancel scheduled changes directly in the portal

If return_url is not provided, FRONTEND_URL from settings will be used as default.

Task cost estimate request

Supports three scenarios:
1. Empty request: Use default template with mock product data (for general cost estimation)
2. New task from template: Provide task_template_id and/or product_id
3. Task copy (re-execution): Provide task_id
